title: Use a flat xref table for FTR dependencies
rationale: Flat strings round-trip cleanly through the current JSON and ODS tooling.

title: Keep the project_memory workspace pinned to the current package release
rationale: Document the current repo pin as spreadsheet-handling==0.2.0 for the next release cycle.

title: Visible spreadsheet payload is a string substrate; typing is opt-in metadata
rationale: Closed the 0.2 release-readiness cutline gate by architectural decision rather than code: both backend parsers already stringify cell payload at the table-block boundary, so the string contract is a chosen narrowness, not forced ignorance. Typing stays opt-in via the registry-declared cell_codecs family. Three non-disruptive migration paths and five revisit criteria are recorded in the ADR.

title: Reset domain/meta around a Functional-Model-derived bridge DSL
rationale: After the June-2026 requirement inquiry and Functional Model promotion, the implemented domain/meta surface was judged materially larger and more sedimented than the Functional Model requires. The accepted direction is to tolerate breaking domain/meta changes while external adoption is low, derive a smaller bridge DSL from Functional Model use cases, preserve useful adapter/parser/renderer boundaries, use consumers as coverage checks rather than golden masters, allow targeted deletion of obvious duplicate paths, and avoid recreating the current registry-scale surface under new names.

title: Pause Review 005 domain-contract/meta-reference implementation work after Slice 1d
rationale: The slice sequence proved the intended control-loop mode; further registry expansion should wait for a concrete failing contract or later review, not continue by default.

title: Use validate_references.rules_path as the first consumer-operability slice
rationale: rules_path removes duplicated reference-rule declarations without introducing a broad configuration DSL, a ruleset registry, or a plugin purge.

title: Guard parser canonical writes before adapter/render implementation work
rationale: A parser canonical-write allowlist protects persistent presentation payload and documents the boundary before any adapter/render rewrite or broader meta simplification.
